cere: noun. A fleshy, waxy area at the base of the upper beak in certain birds.
cero: noun. A large scombroid food fish (Scomberomorus regalis) found chiefly in the West Indies.
cor: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Cornish.
core: noun. In general usage, an essential part of a thing surrounded by other essential things.
corer: noun. A utensil for removing the core from apples and similar fruit or vegetables.
corvee: noun. Unpaid labor required by a feudal lord.
cory: noun. Any fish belonging to the genus Corydoras, a group of South American freshwater catfish.
coyer: adjective. comparative form of coy: more coy
crore: noun. ten million (10⁷): 10,000,000, that is, with Indian digit grouping, 1,00,00,000.
cry: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Cori.
err: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Erre.
every: determiner. All of a countable group (considered individually), without exception.
eye: noun. An organ through which animals see (“perceive surroundings via light”).
eyer: noun. One who eyes someone or something.
eyre: noun. A journey taken by certain Medieval English itinerant judges (justices in eyre)
orc: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Orma.
ore: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Orejón.
oyer: noun. A hearing in a civil case which is based on the content of a document, in which the plaintiff is required to produce the document.
recover: verb. To get back, to regain (a physical thing; in astronomy and navigation, sight of a thing or a signal).
recovery: noun. The act or process of regaining or repossession of something lost.
ree: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Rejang Kayan.
rover: noun. One who roves: a person or animal that travels around, especially over a wide area, without a fixed destination; a nomad, a roamer, a
rye: noun. A grain used extensively in Europe for making bread, beer, and (now generally) for animal fodder.
vee: noun. The name of the Latin script letter V / v.
veer: verb. To let out (a sail-line), to allow (a sheet) to run out.
veery: noun. An American thrush (Catharus fuscescens) common in the Northern United States and Canada.
very: adverb. To a great extent or degree.
voe: noun. A narrow sea inlet, particularly in the Shetland Islands, similar to a fjord.
